proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of conditional quantile treatment effects under marginal Rosenbaum-style sensitivity, where the worst-case odds ratio of treatment-assignment is bounded covariate-by-covariate, with closed-form quantile-curve envelopes recovering Manski-Pepper at the trivial bound and point identification under unconfoundedness
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains empty for PartialID per ThmSmith/doc/API.md Section 3, and the banked rejected pid_qte_marginal_sensitivity angles concern finite-support process compatibility and finite-Gamma endpoint-boundary behavior. This revision preserves the distinct threshold-coded Tukey quotient/rank kernel and strengthens the separation witness to an arbitrary finite rank gap, not a relabeling of either rejected kernel.","published_axis":"Closest published prior art is MastenPoirier2020 for breakdown-frontier/non-refutation methodology, Wang2026 for sharp CDF envelopes, process sharpness, interval-hull QTE bounds, and breakdown-frontier inference, and Winkler1988 for finite-moment extreme-point geometry. The revised gap is that Theorem 1 shows claims evaluated against the same pointwise/interval-hull envelope table can require quotient systems with ranks 2 and K+1 for arbitrary finite K, while Conjectures 1-2 ask for the general canonical quotient and exact LP/audit/inference certificate complexity theorem."}
seed_list: Prior work MastenPoirierRen2025 would imply that a general treatment-effect-functional sensitivity framework is enough for QTE. Our seed conjectures that covariate-wise odds-ratio sensitivity admits a sharper closed-form clipping-and-inversion CQTE envelope, because conditioning on x exposes arm-specific likelihood-ratio caps that the global functional statement hides.

Prior work Wang2026 has established sharp CDF bounds under marginal sensitivity. Our seed conjectures that the inverse quantile process has a separate simultaneous-attainment boundary, because pointwise CDF sharpness need not paste into one noncrossing quantile curve.

Prior work DornGuo2023 would predict quantile-balancing optimization as the natural route to sharp sensitivity bounds. Our seed conjectures that binary-threshold events yield an analytic CQTE envelope, because the extremal weights saturate on lower tails and complements without numerical balancing.

Prior work DAmourFranksFeller2019 would predict that QTE sensitivity remains governed by unidentified Tukey-factor selection functions. Our seed conjectures that marginal odds-ratio bounds collapse those functions to two tail-clipping constants, because quantile events reduce the unknown factor to a bounded-weight two-cell problem.

Prior work YadlowskyNamkoongBasuDuchiTian2022 establishes sharp mean-effect sensitivity bounds. Our seed conjectures that conditional quantile effects have a different rank-compatibility obstruction, because extremal means can be pasted without preserving a noncrossing inverse CDF process.

Prior work Tan2006 would predict a distributional sensitivity analysis based on propensity-score departures. Our seed conjectures a sharper modern marginal-sensitivity CDF formula, because the odds-ratio cap can be rewritten as a bounded likelihood-ratio cap on each quantile event.

Prior work ManskiPepper2000 establishes endpoint bounds from monotone shape restrictions. Our seed conjectures that Rosenbaum marginal sensitivity traces a continuous path to those endpoint fillers, because the Gamma-to-infinity limit leaves exactly the observed-arm mixture plus shape-constrained missing mass.

Prior work Firpo2007 establishes QTE point identification under unconfoundedness. Our seed conjectures a phase transition from point ID to Manski-Pepper partial ID as Gamma moves from one to infinity, because the likelihood-ratio interval expands continuously from a singleton to endpoint filling.

Prior work FanPark2010 gives sharp distributional and quantile bounds under limited information. Our seed conjectures that covariate-wise marginal sensitivity yields strictly narrower conditional quantile bounds, because observed-arm lower-tail probabilities impose event-specific likelihood-ratio caps absent from generic limited-information bounds.

Prior work KallusMaoZhou2019 would predict that unobserved-confounding sensitivity for heterogeneous effects can be summarized by CATE-style intervals. Our seed conjectures that CQTE requires a separate rank-process condition, because two arms' extremal quantile curves may be pointwise sharp but jointly incompatible.

Prior work ZhangXuTan2025 develops distributional and quantile balancing for sensitivity analysis. Our seed conjectures that balancing scores have a closed-form population target, because exact tail events identify the extremal balancing functions as clipped indicators.

Prior work ZhaoSmallBhattacharya2019 uses percentile-bootstrap sensitivity for IPW estimators. Our seed conjectures that the population identified set for conditional quantiles is algebraically simpler than the IPW sampling problem, because bounded odds ratios imply closed-form CDF envelopes before inference.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work MastenPoirierRen2025 would imply that a general treatment-effect-functional sensitivity framework is enough for QTE. Our seed conjectures that covariate-wise odds-ratio sensitivity admits a sharper closed-form clipping-and-inversion CQTE envelope, because conditioning on x exposes arm-specific likelihood-ratio caps that the global functional statement hides.

anchor_paper.author: Masten, Poirier, and Ren
anchor_paper.claim: Studies sensitivity analysis for broad treatment-effect functionals, including QTE and distributional effects.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Potentially an 'apply general method to conditional target' pattern, not blocked because the proposed surprise is a closed-form clipping formula and endpoint collapse absent from the general functional framing.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from MastenPoirierRen2025 because QTE is one of their covered functionals.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their result is the closest threat, but the seed isolates covariate-wise arm-specific clipping constants, noncrossing curve sharpness, and Manski-Pepper endpoint recovery as theorem-level claims.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Wang2026 has established sharp CDF bounds under marginal sensitivity. Our seed conjectures that the inverse quantile process has a separate simultaneous-attainment boundary, because pointwise CDF sharpness need not paste into one noncrossing quantile curve.

anchor_paper.author: Wang
anchor_paper.claim: Gives sharp distribution-function bounds under marginal sensitivity models.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not merely CDF-to-QTE target variation; not blocked because the phenomenon is process-level simultaneous attainment after inversion.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Wang2026 because quantiles are just inverses of CDFs.
seed_details.rebuttal: Pointwise inverse bounds are routine, but simultaneous attainment of a whole noncrossing quantile curve and CQTE process compatibility are separate function-valued questions.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DornGuo2023 would predict quantile-balancing optimization as the natural route to sharp sensitivity bounds. Our seed conjectures that binary-threshold events yield an analytic CQTE envelope, because the extremal weights saturate on lower tails and complements without numerical balancing.

anchor_paper.author: Dorn and Guo
anchor_paper.claim: Develops sharp marginal-sensitivity analysis through quantile balancing and practical estimation.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Not an extension of a method to a new target; not blocked because it replaces optimization with a claimed analytic saturation theorem for tail events.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from DornGuo2023 because their quantile-balancing problem already computes the sharp bound.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed concerns the closed-form population envelope and quantile-curve inversion, not the algorithmic estimator for generic balancing problems.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work DAmourFranksFeller2019 would predict that QTE sensitivity remains governed by unidentified Tukey-factor selection functions. Our seed conjectures that marginal odds-ratio bounds collapse those functions to two tail-clipping constants, because quantile events reduce the unknown factor to a bounded-weight two-cell problem.

anchor_paper.author: D'Amour, Franks, and Feller
anchor_paper.claim: Uses Tukey factorization for flexible sensitivity analysis and discusses QTE examples.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a - orthogonal to blocklist; the seed contrasts a selection-function sensitivity parameterization with analytic marginal-odds clipping.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Tukey factorization because the selection function can always be optimized over tail events.
seed_details.rebuttal: Tukey factorization does not by itself give the bounded-likelihood-ratio clipping constants or sharp conditional quantile envelope.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work YadlowskyNamkoongBasuDuchiTian2022 establishes sharp mean-effect sensitivity bounds. Our seed conjectures that conditional quantile effects have a different rank-compatibility obstruction, because extremal means can be pasted without preserving a noncrossing inverse CDF process.

anchor_paper.author: Yadlowsky, Namkoong, Basu, Duchi, and Tian
anchor_paper.claim: Gives sharp bounds and inference for treatment effects under marginal sensitivity.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Could look like 'derive QTE from ATE'; not blocked because the claimed novelty is a rank/process obstruction hidden by mean functionals.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from their sharp weight characterization by replacing Y with indicators.
seed_details.rebuttal: Indicator replacement gives pointwise CDF bounds, but not simultaneous quantile-curve sharpness or CQTE arm-coupling compatibility.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Tan2006 would predict a distributional sensitivity analysis based on propensity-score departures. Our seed conjectures a sharper modern marginal-sensitivity CDF formula, because the odds-ratio cap can be rewritten as a bounded likelihood-ratio cap on each quantile event.

anchor_paper.author: Tan
anchor_paper.claim: Develops a distributional approach to causal sensitivity analysis using propensity scores.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens/modernizes an older assumption pattern; not blocked because the proposed phenomenon is exact two-cell clipping and sharp quantile inversion under marginal odds ratios.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Tan2006 because distributional sensitivity already treats CDFs.
seed_details.rebuttal: Tan2006 is foundational but does not state the covariate-wise closed-form CQTE envelope or Manski-Pepper endpoint interpolation.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ManskiPepper2000 establishes endpoint bounds from monotone shape restrictions. Our seed conjectures that Rosenbaum marginal sensitivity traces a continuous path to those endpoint fillers, because the Gamma-to-infinity limit leaves exactly the observed-arm mixture plus shape-constrained missing mass.

anchor_paper.author: Manski and Pepper
anchor_paper.claim: Introduces monotone instrumental variables and shape restrictions for partial identification.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern; not blocked because the endpoint transition is a phase-limit check tying two sensitivity regimes, not just broader assumptions.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Manski-Pepper because Gamma infinity simply removes unconfoundedness.
seed_details.rebuttal: The exact limit must preserve observed-arm mixture mass and impose shape restrictions only on missing fillers; that interpolation is not stated in Manski-Pepper.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Firpo2007 establishes QTE point identification under unconfoundedness. Our seed conjectures a phase transition from point ID to Manski-Pepper partial ID as Gamma moves from one to infinity, because the likelihood-ratio interval expands continuously from a singleton to endpoint filling.

anchor_paper.author: Firpo
anchor_paper.claim: Gives efficient semiparametric estimation of QTE under unconfoundedness.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern; not blocked because the seed asks for exact sensitivity path and endpoint behavior, not just weakened unconfoundedness.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Firpo2007 plus standard sensitivity models because Gamma=1 is unconfoundedness.
seed_details.rebuttal: The Gamma=1 endpoint is immediate, but the full closed-form path and Gamma=infinity endpoint are not in Firpo2007.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work FanPark2010 gives sharp distributional and quantile bounds under limited information. Our seed conjectures that covariate-wise marginal sensitivity yields strictly narrower conditional quantile bounds, because observed-arm lower-tail probabilities impose event-specific likelihood-ratio caps absent from generic limited-information bounds.

anchor_paper.author: Fan and Park
anchor_paper.claim: Derives sharp bounds on distributions and quantiles of treatment effects under limited information.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a - orthogonal to blocklist; the seed identifies a strict-narrowing phenomenon from marginal sensitivity relative to generic limited-information bounds.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from FanPark2010 because their sharp quantile bounds cover arbitrary partial information.
seed_details.rebuttal: Fan-Park frames generic distributional bounds, but the covariate-wise odds-ratio cap yields a different closed-form conditional CDF envelope.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work KallusMaoZhou2019 would predict that unobserved-confounding sensitivity for heterogeneous effects can be summarized by CATE-style intervals. Our seed conjectures that CQTE requires a separate rank-process condition, because two arms' extremal quantile curves may be pointwise sharp but jointly incompatible.

anchor_paper.author: Kallus, Mao, and Zhou
anchor_paper.claim: Develops interval estimation for individual-level causal effects under unobserved confounding.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target-variation pattern; not blocked because the surprise is rank-process incompatibility, not simply a quantile version of a mean interval.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from individual-level intervals by evaluating at distributional cutoffs.
seed_details.rebuttal: Individual mean-effect intervals do not determine marginal quantile curves or their cross-arm rank coupling.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ZhangXuTan2025 develops distributional and quantile balancing for sensitivity analysis. Our seed conjectures that balancing scores have a closed-form population target, because exact tail events identify the extremal balancing functions as clipped indicators.

anchor_paper.author: Zhang, Xu, and Tan
anchor_paper.claim: Develops distributional and quantile balancing for sensitivity analysis.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Could be method-to-closed-form; not blocked because the proposed new term is the exact clipped-indicator population envelope for every tail threshold.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from their balancing equations because they are designed for quantile sensitivity.
seed_details.rebuttal: Balancing equations do not automatically identify the closed-form sharp CQTE envelope or endpoint interpolation theorem.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ZhaoSmallBhattacharya2019 uses percentile-bootstrap sensitivity for IPW estimators. Our seed conjectures that the population identified set for conditional quantiles is algebraically simpler than the IPW sampling problem, because bounded odds ratios imply closed-form CDF envelopes before inference.

anchor_paper.author: Zhao, Small, and Bhattacharya
anchor_paper.claim: Studies sensitivity analysis for inverse probability weighting estimators under marginal sensitivity.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: n/a - orthogonal to blocklist; the seed separates population sharpness from estimator bootstrap behavior.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from their IPW sensitivity formulation by taking indicator outcomes.
seed_details.rebuttal: Indicator outcomes give pointwise tail means, not conditional quantile-curve envelopes or CQTE compatibility statements.

proposed_from.literature_map: Rosenbaum1995 (Springer, Observational Studies, Rosenbaum1995) sets the odds-ratio hidden-bias scale; tension: classical matched-study sensitivity does not give conditional quantile-curve envelopes. Tan2006 (JASA, Tan2006) develops distributional propensity-score sensitivity; open gap: sharp closed-form conditional QTE envelopes. ManskiPepper2000 (Econometrica, ManskiPepper2000) gives monotone-IV and monotone-response partial-ID logic; tension: no continuous path from Rosenbaum sensitivity to the endpoint. Firpo2007 (Econometrica, Firpo2007) gives point-identified QTE under unconfoundedness; endpoint target for Gamma=1. FanPark2010 (Econometric Theory, FanPark2010) studies sharp distribution/quantile bounds under limited information; gap: not marginal odds-ratio sensitivity with covariate-wise Gamma. DAmourFranksFeller2019 (JASA, DAmourFranksFeller2019) uses Tukey factorization for flexible sensitivity with QTE examples; tension: Bayesian/non-sharp sensitivity rather than closed-form sharp envelopes. ZhaoSmallBhattacharya2019 (JRSSB, ZhaoSmallBhattacharya2019) develops marginal sensitivity for IPW; gap: mean/IPW focus, not conditional quantile curves. KallusMaoZhou2019 (AISTATS, KallusMaoZhou2019) gives intervals for individual-level mean effects under unobserved confounding; gap: heterogeneous means, not distributional rank/quantile processes. YadlowskyNamkoongBasuDuchiTian2022 (Annals of Statistics, YadlowskyNamkoongBasuDuchiTian2022) gives sharp marginal-sensitivity ATE/CATE-style bounds; gap: no CQTE curve. DornGuo2023 (JASA, DornGuo2023) gives sharp practical marginal-sensitivity bounds via quantile balancing; tension: computational balancing versus explicit conditional quantile inversion. ZhangXuTan2025 (JASA, ZhangXuTan2025) studies distributional and quantile balancing; open issue: whether exact analytic CQTE envelopes can replace optimization. MastenPoirierRen2025 (working paper, MastenPoirierRen2025) is the closest broad QTE/DTE sensitivity threat; novelty must be the covariate-wise clipping formula plus process and endpoint claims. Wang2026 (arXiv, Wang2026) studies sharp distribution-function bounds under marginal sensitivity; open gap: process-level quantile inversion and CQTE compatibility.
